title: Auto-Fill Two Email Columns When New Item is Created

posts.author: GG95 (Sharon Guli)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I’m looking for a way to automatically populate two email columns when a new item is created. Ideally, each new item should have predefined email addresses in both columns without manual input.

Is there a built-in automation for this, or would it require an integration? Any suggestions would be appreciated!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Is the email for a customer or a user? Or is it predefined?

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml: You can use the built-in automation feature in Monday.com to automatically populate email columns with predefined email addresses when a new item is created. If you need further assistance or would like to schedule a call, please visit our website at Thewild6.com.

posts.author: Timothy7 (Timothy Kariuki | CreativeBits LLC)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can use Make.com to automate this process by watching the board for new item creations and automatically prefilling the two email columns with the desired values.

Steps to Set Up the Automation:

Monitor the Board – Set up Make.com to watch for new items created in the board.
Auto-Fill Email Columns – Once a new item is detected, update the two email columns with the predefined email addresses.

This automation ensures efficiency by eliminating manual data entry and maintaining consistency in your records.

Let me know if you need further assistance!

posts.author: GG95 (Sharon Guli)
posts.cookedHtml: I cannot find this specific action automation feature.

posts.author: GG95 (Sharon Guli)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Desiree,
I want it to be predefined.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Add two email columns, then use the Default Item Values to fill in what you want it to be
